OSConfig
  • macOS Overview

macOS Overview

macOS is a Unix-based operating system developed by Apple Inc. for its Mac hardware lineup. It combines a polished graphical user interface with a stable Unix foundation, making it popular among developers, creative professionals, and general users. macOS integrates tightly with Apple’s ecosystem, offering seamless interaction with devices such as iPhones, iPads, and Apple Watches.

The primary advantage of macOS is its stability, ease of use, and strong ecosystem integration. It provides a consistent user experience, high-quality native applications, and robust support for development tools. However, it is limited to Apple hardware, which can be expensive and less customizable than typical PC-based systems. Additionally, while it offers Unix capabilities, it does not provide the same level of system-level control and openness as most Linux distributions.
